name: create_file
description: Create an empty file at path. Prefer create_file(path) then write_file(path, content) so you can write in one or more steps. Omit content or pass empty for create-only. If overwrite is false, the file must not exist.

content.description: Optional initial content; omit or empty for create-only.

name: write_file
description: Overwrite a file with new content. Creates the file if it does not exist. Use ONLY when replacing the entire file content. Do NOT use for partial edits (use apply_patch instead). For .gd files: the file already has one 'extends ClassName' at the top; do not add another extends line.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/player.gd).

name: append_to_file
description: Append content to the end of a file. Creates the file if it does not exist. Use ONLY for incremental writes at the end of a file.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/player.gd).

content.description: Content to append to the file.

name: apply_patch
description: Edit a file by replacing the first occurrence of old_string with new_string, or pass a unified diff. Use ONLY for small, targeted edits to existing files. Do NOT use for full rewrites (use write_file). For .gd files: do not add a second 'extends' line; the script already has one at the top.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/player.gd).

old_string.description: Exact text to find and replace (omit if using diff).

diff.description: Optional unified diff string instead of old_string/new_string.

name: create_script
description: Create a new GDScript or C# script file with one extends line and initial content. Use template (e.g. character_2d) to fill boilerplate so you only supply initial_content for the unique logic. The created file will have exactly one 'extends' at the top; never add another.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/player.gd).

extends_class.description: Base class, e.g. Node, CharacterBody2D (ignored if template is set).

initial_content.description: Optional body content; with template this is the unique logic only.

name: create_node
description: Add a new node to a scene. Executes in the Godot editor: opens the scene, adds the node, saves. ALWAYS attach to the current scene: omit scene_path (or use 'current'). parent_path defaults to /root. Match the scene dimension: in a 2D scene use Node2D, CharacterBody2D, Sprite2D, CollisionShape2D, etc.; in a 3D scene use Node3D, CharacterBody3D, MeshInstance3D, etc. Do NOT use 3D types in a 2D scene or vice versa.

scene_path.description: Optional. res:// path to scene, e.g. res://main.tscn (or use 'current' for the open scene).

parent_path.description: Node path of parent in scene; default /root (scene root).

node_type.description: Built-in Godot class only: Node, Node2D, Button, Label, CharacterBody2D, Sprite2D, etc.

node_name.description: Optional name for the new node.

name: modify_attribute
description: Set an attribute/property on a target. Use target_type to choose: 'node' = property on a node in a scene (scene_path, node_path, attribute, value); 'import' = key in the .import file [params] for a resource (path, attribute, value). Examples: node position, text; import compress (SVG lossless), mipmaps.

value.description: New value (number, string, bool, or [x,y] for vectors).

scene_path.description: Required if target_type=node. Scene file path starting with res:// (e.g., res://main.tscn).

node_path.description: Required if target_type=node. Path to the node inside the scene, e.g. /root/Sprite

path.description: Required if target_type=import. Resource path, e.g. res://icon.svg

name: read_file
description: Read the full contents of a project file. Use this whenever you need to see the current content of a file (e.g. before editing, or when the user asks what's in a file). You will receive the file content in the tool result.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/player.gd).

name: delete_file
description: Delete a file from the project. Do NOT use this unless requested.

path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://scripts/old.gd).

name: list_directory
description: List files and folders in a directory under res://.

path.description: Directory path, e.g. res:// or res://scripts

name: search_files
description: Search for a text query inside project files under res:// (grep: finds files containing the text).

query.description: Text to search for inside files.

name: list_files
description: List file paths under res:// by optional extension(s), without searching file contents. Use this to find all files of a type (e.g. all .svg, .png, .tscn). Returns paths only.

path.description: Directory to list, e.g. res:// or res://assets

extensions.description: Filter by extension, e.g. ['.svg'], ['.png','.jpg']. Omit for all files.

name: read_import_options
description: Read the .import file for a resource (e.g. res://icon.svg). Returns the file content so you can see current import options. Import options control how Godot imports assets (e.g. SVG compression, texture mipmaps).

path.description: Resource path, e.g. res://icon.svg (the .import file read is path.import).

name: lint_file
description: Run the Godot script linter on a project file (e.g. res://player.gd). Use when the user asks to lint a file or check for errors. The linter output is shown in the editor.

path.description: Project path to the script, e.g. res://scripts/foo.gd

name: project_structure
description: List indexed project file paths under a prefix (from the repo index). Use to see what files exist without reading them (e.g. 'where is Player?' or project layout).

prefix.description: res:// prefix, e.g. res:// or res://scripts

max_depth.description: Max path depth (segment count). Omit for no limit.

name: find_scripts_by_extends
description: Find script files that extend a given class (e.g. CharacterBody2D, Node). Returns paths to .gd/.cs files that contain 'extends ClassName'.

extends_class.description: Class name, e.g. CharacterBody2D, Control, Node

name: find_references_to
description: Find files that reference a given path (e.g. a scene or script). Uses the project index to return paths that reference the target (instances, scripts, res:// refs).

res_path.description: Target path, e.g. res://player.tscn or res://scripts/player.gd

name: get_recent_changes
description: Return the last N edit events (what files were recently created/modified by the AI). Use to see what was just edited.

limit.description: Max number of events to return.

name: grep_search
description: Search project files with a regex or exact pattern. Returns file path, line number, and line text for each match. Use for symbol/pattern search (e.g. function names, class refs). For simple substring 'which files contain X' use search_files.

pattern.description: Regex pattern or literal text to search for (or use query as alias).

use_regex.description: If true, pattern is a regex; else literal.

name: fetch_url
description: Fetch the content of a URL via HTTP GET. Use to look up external documentation (e.g. Godot docs, API pages) when the user asks for docs or API info; this replaces searching an internal doc index.

url.description: Full URL to fetch (must be http:// or https://).

name: run_terminal_command
description: Run a shell command on the user's machine. Captures stdout, stderr, and exit code. Use for running scripts, godot --headless, or build commands.

name: run_godot_headless
description: Run Godot headlessly with a scene or script path. Captures stdout/stderr and exit code. Enables write-run-observe-fix loop. Use scene_path (or script_path as alias) to specify what to run.

scene_path.description: res:// path to scene or script to run (or use script_path as alias).

name: run_scene
description: Run a Godot scene headlessly and capture output/errors. Critical for test-driven agent loop.

scene_path.description: Project path starting with res:// (e.g., res://main.tscn).

name: get_node_tree
description: Get the scene tree structure (node names, types, hierarchy) for the current open scene or a given .tscn path.

scene_path.description: Optional. res://path/to/scene.tscn; omit for current open scene.

name: get_signals
description: List available signals for a node type or script (name, arguments). Use to reason about signal connections. Provide at least one of node_type (built-in) or script_path (res:// to script).

node_type.description: Built-in node type, e.g. Button, CharacterBody2D.

script_path.description: res:// path to script to inspect for signals.

name: connect_signal
description: Connect a signal on a node to a callable (e.g. another node's method).

node_path.description: Path to the node in the scene.

callable_target.description: Target node path and method, e.g. ../Player/on_clicked.

name: get_export_vars
description: List @export variables for a script or node (name, type, default). Essential for understanding inspector-configurable state.

scene_path.description: Scene path if inspecting a node's script.

node_path.description: Node path in scene if inspecting attached script.

name: search_asset_library
description: Search the Godot Asset Library for addons/plugins by keyword. Returns asset title, author, support level, browse URL. Use when the user asks for a plugin or addon for a feature.

filter.description: Search keyword (or use query as alias).

name: get_project_settings
description: Read project.godot settings (key-value by section). Use to see display, rendering, or other config.

name: get_autoloads
description: List autoloaded singletons from project.godot (name and path). Agents need to know what is globally available.

name: get_input_map
description: Read the input map from project.godot (action names and bound keys). Use when writing input handling code.

name: check_errors
description: Return the current editor Errors/Warnings panel content (script errors, etc.). Godot equivalent of linter output.
